rationale: The citing paper uses Dhar and Wertenbroch (2000) to ground its core hedonic-versus-utilitarian distinction, including explicit construct definitions in Study 1. It also cites the paper for the claim that products can be positioned as either experiential or functional, which directly supports the design of its positioning manipulations. These are central setup elements for the hypotheses and mediation logic, not just a passing background mention.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Dhar and Wertenbroch (2000) as conceptual groundwork for distinguishing utilitarian and hedonic benefits. That foundation is used to build and justify the functional versus experiential positioning framework tested in the incongruent-form studies.
